intro: Rinorea elliptica, also known as the African dog rose or African dog wood, is a plant species in the family Violaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which have been attributed to its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The bioactive constituents of Rinorea elliptica include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, antitumor, and anti-histaminic activities. Rinorea elliptica is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be found in various herbal remedies such as capsules and tinctures.
